An anthropomorphic Alderson phantom equipped with 50 thermoluminescent dosimeters (TLDs) was scanned using different CTCA protocols and an uncomplicated diagnosticThe study compares radiation doses from three computed tomography coronary angiography (CTCA) protocols—sequential ECG-triggered, retrospectively gated spiral, and high-pitch spiral—as well as catheter coronary angiography (CCA), using an anthropomorphic phantom with 50 thermoluminescent dosimeters (TLDs). 1. Protocol Types: - Sequential ECG-Triggered: Scans are performed in a stepwise manner, timed according to the heart's electrical activity. - Retrospectively Gated Spiral: Data is collected continuously during scanning and sorted post-procedure based on ECG signals. - High-Pitch Spiral: A faster helical scanning technique that may reduce radiation by capturing data efficiently in a single pass. 2. Study Aim: To determine which CTCA protocol delivers the lowest radiation dose, crucial for patient safety. The use of an Alderson phantom provides realistic and consistent measurements across different body areas using 50 TLDs. 3. Comparison with CCA: Catheter coronary angiography (CCA) serves as a control to compare CT methods' radiation efficiency. While CCA is invasive, it offers insights into how non-invasive CT techniques perform in terms of radiation exposure. 4. Potential Findings and Considerations: - High-pitch
